questions.q: Who killed Ravana in the great war?
questions.explanation: Rama himself slew Ravana in their final duel on the battlefield of Lanka.

questions.q: What divine weapon did Rama use to finally kill Ravana?
questions.explanation: Rama loosed the Brahmastra, the supreme weapon, which pierced Ravana's heart.

questions.q: Ravana was the king of which city?
questions.explanation: Ravana ruled over the golden city of Lanka as its demon-king.

questions.q: How many heads is Ravana traditionally depicted as having?
questions.explanation: Ravana is famously known as Dashagriva, the ten-headed demon-king.

questions.q: Whose abduction by Ravana led to the war that ended in his death?
questions.explanation: Ravana abducted Sita, Rama's wife, sparking the war that brought about his downfall.

questions.q: What weapon did Rama use throughout the war to fight Ravana?
questions.explanation: Rama was a master archer who fought with his bow and a quiver of arrows.

questions.q: Who was Rama's loyal brother who fought beside him against Ravana's forces?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana accompanied Rama throughout the exile and the war against Ravana.

questions.q: The duel that ended Ravana's life took place during the war for which city?
questions.explanation: The climactic battle was fought outside the gates of Lanka, Ravana's kingdom.

questions.q: After Ravana's death, what was the world said to be freed from?
questions.explanation: Ravana's slaying freed the world of his tyranny and oppression of the righteous.

questions.q: What army helped Rama wage the war against Ravana?
questions.explanation: Rama was aided by the vast vanara (monkey) army led by Sugriva and Hanuman.

questions.q: Who advised Rama of the secret that the source of Ravana's life lay in his navel?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous brother who joined Rama, revealed the secret of striking Ravana's navel.

questions.q: Where did the Brahmastra strike to finally end Ravana's life?
questions.explanation: In Valmiki's account the Brahmastra pierced Ravana's heart, drinking his blood and felling him.

questions.q: Why could Rama not kill Ravana simply by cutting off his heads?
options: He had no heads to cut
questions.explanation: Each time Rama severed a head, a new one grew in its place until the Brahmastra was used.

questions.q: Which sage gave Rama the Brahmastra (the weapon of Brahma) used against Ravana?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishwamitra had earlier imparted to Rama the divine astras, including the Brahmastra.

questions.q: According to tradition, what hymn did sage Agastya teach Rama before the final battle with Ravana?
questions.explanation: Agastya taught Rama the Aditya Hridayam, a hymn to the Sun, to gain strength for slaying Ravana.

questions.q: Who was Ravana's son, a mighty warrior, slain by Lakshmana before Ravana's own death?
questions.explanation: Indrajit, also called Meghanada, was Ravana's most powerful son, killed by Lakshmana earlier in the war.

questions.q: Which giant brother of Ravana was slain by Rama before the final duel with Ravana?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna, the colossal sleeping brother of Ravana, was killed by Rama earlier in the battle.

questions.q: Whose chariot did the gods send for Rama so he could fight Ravana on equal footing?
questions.explanation: Indra sent his celestial chariot driven by his charioteer Matali so Rama would not fight on foot.

questions.q: Who was Ravana's chief queen who mourned his death?
questions.explanation: Mandodari, Ravana's principal queen, delivered a sorrowful lament over his fallen body.

questions.q: After Ravana's death, who was crowned the new king of Lanka by Rama?
questions.explanation: Rama installed the virtuous Vibhishana, Ravana's brother and ally, as the new king of Lanka.

questions.q: What did Rama instruct Lakshmana to do regarding the dying Ravana, to learn from him?
options: To approach and learn statecraft and wisdom from him
questions.explanation: Rama sent Lakshmana to the dying Ravana to learn lessons of statecraft and wisdom from the learned demon-king.

questions.q: Ravana had earned a boon that he could not be killed by which beings?
options: Gods, demons, and other celestial beings
questions.explanation: Ravana's boon from Brahma made him invulnerable to gods, demons and divine beings, but he scorned men, so Rama as a man could slay him.

questions.q: In Valmiki's Ramayana, the final duel between Rama and Ravana is described in which kanda (book)?
questions.explanation: The war and Ravana's slaying are narrated in the Yuddha Kanda, the sixth book of the Ramayana.

questions.q: Which divine craftsman is said to have forged the Brahmastra arrow described in Rama's final shot?
questions.explanation: The supreme arrow was said to be fashioned by Brahma through Vishwakarma, charged with the power of the gods.

questions.q: According to the Yuddha Kanda, what were the feathers of Rama's final Brahmastra arrow associated with?
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes the decisive arrow as embodying Agni and the Sun in its tip, with the wind in its feathers, blazing with cosmic power.

questions.q: What ominous portents are said to have appeared in Lanka as Ravana approached his death?
options: Evil omens such as bleeding idols and unnatural darkness
questions.explanation: Valmiki records dire omens in Lanka, including weeping and bleeding images and unnatural gloom, foretelling Ravana's doom.

questions.q: Who was Ravana's grandfather, the sage from whom his Brahminical lineage descended?
questions.explanation: Ravana was the grandson of the sage Pulastya, one of the mind-born sons of Brahma, giving him Brahmin ancestry.

questions.q: On which day in popular tradition is the slaying of Ravana celebrated?
questions.explanation: Vijayadashami, or Dussehra, commemorates Rama's victory over Ravana, marked by burning effigies of the demon-king.

questions.q: What was Ravana's original name before he gained his epithet meaning 'one who makes the universe roar'?
options: Ravana from the roar he caused under Mount Kailasa
questions.explanation: Shiva named him 'Ravana' after he was crushed under Mount Kailasa and let out a terrible roar, the name meaning one who roars.

questions.q: After Ravana fell, who first lamented over the body before the formal funeral rites were performed?
options: Mandodari and the women of the harem
questions.explanation: Mandodari and the grieving women of Ravana's harem came out to lament over his slain body before his cremation.
